name: D+Q bone RCT 20-week CTx synthesis compatibility
question: Can the 20-week CTx result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Between-group CTx percent-change effect with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because only one result is available and the effect record is p-value-only.

pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count and extraction completeness. This endpoint-specific group has one CTx result from one trial, while the pooling requirement is at least two compatible results. The registry record contains arm medians, full ranges, and a Wilcoxon p-value, but no extractable between-group effect value or CI, standard error, or variance.
non_pooling_reason: Do not pool this primary CTx endpoint until at least one additional compatible CTx result is available and every included result has an effect value, uncertainty estimate, comparison, sample size, and required group-value fields.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The result is a p-value-only overall CTx comparison with group medians and full ranges; full range is not a variance estimate, and only one endpoint result is available.

self_healing_actions: Keep this endpoint group in pooling_blocked state until effect uncertainty and at least one additional compatible CTx result are available.
Route p-value-only CTx extraction to extraction_refresh if full text or supplements provide a between-group estimate with uncertainty.

provenance.note: Posted primary CTx outcome group values and D+Q versus untreated-control p-value.
Abstract confirms no significant overall CTx difference between groups.
